title: Filtering, Three-Valued Logic, Patterns, Ordering, and Pagination · Data Contract Brief: Pattern Filtering
summary: A queue view must include the correct unresolved tickets, handle missing owners, and remain deterministic across pages. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql pattern filtering increment in a deterministic queue-filter query suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply LIKE and explicit escaping or case rules without confusing substring search with equality.
Use IS NULL, IS NOT NULL, COALESCE, and NULLIF only when their business meaning is justified.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Return unresolved high-or-critical tickets plus unowned work from the seeded database. Expose ticket_id, a readable owner label, age bucket 3, and deterministic severity/opened ordering; then test a NULL owner and tied timestamp. A defensible solution applies Pattern Filtering, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a deterministic queue-filter query suite.

content.code:
SELECT ticket_id,
       COALESCE(CAST(owner_id AS TEXT), 'unassigned') AS owner,
       CASE severity WHEN 'critical' THEN 1 WHEN 'high' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END AS severity_order
FROM tickets
WHERE resolved_at IS NULL
ORDER BY severity_order, opened_at, ticket_id
LIMIT 6;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Pattern Filtering evidence · Theory Pattern Filtering
steps.instruction:

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-pattern-filtering-cm5d69
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT ticket_id, owner_id, owner_id = 1 AS owner_is_one
FROM tickets
WHERE owner_id = 1 OR owner_id IS NULL
ORDER BY ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
